title: Next.js Server Actions CSRF bypass: Origin: null treated as missing instead of cross-origin
description: In handleAction() in packages/next/src/server/app-render/action-handler.ts, the Server Actions CSRF protection derives originDomain from the request's Origin header with `typeof originHeader === 'string' && originHeader !== 'null'`. An Origin value of the literal string 'null' (sent by opaque/privacy-sensitive browser contexts such as sandboxed iframes, data: URLs, and certain redirects) therefore falls into the `: undefined` branch, making originDomain undefined. The CSRF check then enters the `if (!originDomain)` branch, which merely records a warning ('Missing `origin` header ...') and lets the request proceed, instead of treating 'null' as a distinct cross-origin value to be validated against the host or the experimental.serverActions.allowedOrigins allowlist. As a result, an attacker can induce a victim browser to submit Server Actions from a sandboxed/opaque context, executing state-changing actions with the victim's credentials (CSRF).
